description_long: This is a compact, twin-engine propeller airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a low-wing configuration. The aircraft is painted primarily gray, with prominent orange bands on the nose, wingtips, and tail, and features roundels and insignia on the wings and tail fin, suggesting a military or training role. The wings are straight and low-mounted, with visible landing gear beneath each wing and a tricycle gear arrangement. The tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer with a tricolor pattern and a horizontal stabilizer mounted low. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, likely accommodating one or two crew members. The overall design is functional and robust, suitable for short-range flights or training missions.
description: A compact, twin-propeller, low-wing airplane with gray and orange livery, military insignia, and a conventional tail. Features an enclosed forward cockpit and tricycle landing gear.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full wingspan, orange bands, and roundels on the wings and tail.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the twin propellers, nose cone, and the forward cockpit canopy.
description_view_2: Bottom-up view revealing the landing gear, underwing details, and the orange band on the tail.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the tail section, tricolor vertical stabilizer, and horizontal stabilizer configuration.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the wings, each with a propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and low-mounted horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units under the wings and a single nose gear, all fixed and visible.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single canopy, likely accommodating one or two seats.
